# Codex.app Account Switcher

Codex.app Account Switcher is an unofficial local macOS CLI and launcher for switching the active Codex.app account snapshot. It is designed for users who already maintain multiple user-owned Codex / ChatGPT auth snapshots locally.

## What it does

- Reads local account pool metadata from `~/.codex/accounts/registry.json`.
- Reads local auth snapshots from `~/.codex/accounts/*.auth.json`.
- Refreshes tokens and reads live ChatGPT/Codex usage when possible.
- Selects an account whose live usage is readable and whose remaining quota passes configured thresholds.
- Writes the selected snapshot to `~/.codex/auth.json`.
- Optionally restarts `/Applications/Codex.app` so the desktop app loads the new auth state.
- Imports candidate auth JSON from `codex-auth`, `codex-sub2api`, or flat token JSON only after live validation.
- Provides update checking and self-update for the installed local scripts.

## What it is not

- Not an OpenAI product.
- Not a hosted account service.
- Not a quota bypass tool.
- Not a credential provider.
- Not a server, Docker app, database app, or cloud deployment.

## Primary audience

Developers and advanced Codex.app users on macOS who understand local auth files and want a safer repeatable workflow for switching between their own local Codex / ChatGPT account snapshots.

## Technology

Bash, Node.js ESM, jq, curl, macOS open/osascript, local filesystem operations under `~/.codex`.

## Important files

- `README.md`: main bilingual project overview, installation, quick start, limitations.
- `codex-app-hot-switch.sh`: high-level switch and optional relaunch flow.
- `codex-auth-smart-switch.sh`: live usage refresh, account selection, cleanup logic.
- `codex-auth-import-json.mjs`: source JSON import and live validation.
- `codex-auth-load-free.mjs`: local Free-account source scan helper.
- `codex-app-relaunch.sh`: macOS Codex.app restart helper.
- `scripts/install.sh`: installer and CLI wrapper creator.
- `scripts/check-updates.sh`: project and dependency update checker.
- `docs/security.md`: credential boundaries and public repository safety.
- `docs/operations.md`: daily operations and troubleshooting.
- `docs/usage-examples.md`: practical command examples.
- `docs/faq.md`: common limitations and behavior questions.

## Keywords

Codex.app account switcher, Codex account pool, ChatGPT auth snapshot, macOS Codex CLI helper, local auth management, codex-auth workflow, Bash Node.js jq developer tool, Codex desktop relaunch, AI coding account switch workflow.
